In all, 14 casings from a Browning 9 mm Luger and multiple bullets were recovered from the scene of the shooting.
Cameron, the victim, sustained up to nine different bullet wounds, but incredibly he survived and was able to talk to investigators from his bedside.
Though he told investigators that he had no memory of the shooting.
According to court documents, police immediately began interviewing known associates and it was determined that the victim was in contact with Alexia Gagige Mary Cutbank.

Investigators reached out to the Paul Bunyan Drug Task Force in Beltrami County, who was able to confirm that Alexia Cutbank was the sister of Montana Cutbank, who was in jail awaiting trial for allegedly murdering a criminal associate back in November 2020.
As police continued their investigation, it became increasingly clear that this was a mob or organized crime attempted assassination.
Cameron's friends and family believed that he was the intended target of that murder.

According to court documents, Alexia then said that she brought someone else along with her, even mentioning him by name over the phone, Seneca Warrior Steprock.

Alexia Cutbank was taken into custody without incident, and court documents indicate that she was in possession of a loaded 9 mm handgun, the same caliber of handgun used in the attempted hit on Cameron.
After a brief foot pursuit, Seneca Steprock was also taken into custody, but not before he was able to bury his own 9 mm handgun in the snow, which was found later by a K9 unit.
Alexia Cutbank was convicted of first-degree attempted murder and is currently serving a sentence of over 19 years.
Seneca Steprock was convicted of aiding and abetting attempted first-degree murder and being in the possession of a firearm as a convicted felon.
He is currently serving a 20-year sentence.
Cameron was interviewed after both arrests were made, however, he claimed to not remember anything following the shooting or who could have been behind it.
